name: Proto-Central-Semitic
notes: Proto-Central-Semitic (~2500–2000 BCE). Ancestor of: Arabic, Northwest Semitic (Canaanite + Aramaic). Excludes South Semitic (Ethiopic, South Arabian). KEY INNOVATIONS: (1) *ś /ɬ/ begins merging with /s/ (not /ʃ/ — that is the Canaanite reflex); (2) *ṣ́ /ɬˤ/ begins shifting toward /ðˤ/ in the Arabic branch; (3) short vowel reduction in unstressed syllables (morphophonological). Consonant inventory: 29 phonemes (same letters as PSem but with *ś and *ṣ́ in transitional states).
